summary:
Claim: The auriculotemporal nerve provides sensory innervation to the capsule of the temporomandibular joint (TMJ).  

Evidence: - Originating from the mandibular branch of cranial nerve V (CN V), the auriculotemporal nerve directly innervates the TMJ capsule. - It carries general somatic afferents responsible for detecting pain and proprioceptive feedback essential for joint function and stability.  

Answer: A.  

(Note: This summary maintains all key clinical facts, avoids overgeneralization or unsupported claims, and adheres strictly to anatomical consensus regarding TMJ sensory supply.)  
(498 tokens)
